highlights: Conducting vulnerability assessments and penetration tests on web applications and internal infrastructure using tools such as Burp Suite, Nmap, etc.
Identifying and documenting security vulnerabilities with validated proof-of-concept evidence, delivering actionable remediation guidance aligned with industry standards.
Producing structured vulnerability reports with severity classifications to communicate risk clearly to development and security teams.
Developing custom Python-based automation scripts to streamline reconnaissance and testing workflows, improving overall assessment efficiency.
Conducted end-to-end VAPT and reconnaissance on web apps and network infrastructure, successfully uncovering high-severity flaws using tools like Burp Suite and Nmap.
Assisted senior team members in validating exploits and authored detailed, CVSS-scored penetration testing reports with proof-of-concept evidence.
Coordinated 'LOOP: Last One On Peak,' an attack and persistence-based hacking competition simulating real-world adversary tactics.
Provided technical infrastructure assistance and support for various cybersecurity events, including Capture The Flag challenges and specialized hardware lab workshops.
Completed intensive, lab-based training covering ethical hacking methodologies, network penetration testing, and web application security under the OCSP curriculum.
Gained hands-on proficiency with offensive security tools including Metasploit, Burp Suite, Nmap, Wireshark, Gobuster, and WPScan across controlled lab environments.
Studied Active Directory attack paths including enumeration, Kerberoasting, Pass-the-Hash, and privilege escalation techniques within Windows environments.
Earned the Offenso Certified Security Professional (OCSP) certification upon successful completion of practical examination.
